{"id":"mission-control-vug.1","title":"Define Profile types and schema","description":"Define the TypeScript types for Claude Code Profiles and add Zod validation schemas.

## What to do
1. Add `Profile` interface to `mission-control/scripts/daemon/types.ts`:
   ```ts
   export interface Profile {
     id: string;           // unique identifier (e.g., 'default', 'openai-compat', 'local-llm')
     name: string;         // display name
     description?: string; // optional description
     env: Record<string, string>; // environment variables to set when spawning Claude Code
   }
   ```

2. Add `ProfilesConfig` to `DaemonConfig` type:
   ```ts
   profiles: {
     definitions: Profile[];
     defaultProfileId: string; // defaults to 'default'
   };
   ```

3. Add `Profile` type to `mission-control/src/lib/types.ts` (mirrors daemon types for the frontend)

4. Add Zod schemas to `mission-control/src/lib/validations.ts`:
   - profileCreateSchema
   - profileUpdateSchema
   - Update daemonConfigUpdateSchema to include profiles section

5. The 'default' profile should have an empty env object (plain Claude Code behavior)

## Files to modify
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/types.ts
- mission-control/src/lib/types.ts
- mission-control/src/lib/validations.ts

## Acceptance criteria
- Profile interface defined with id, name, description, env fields
- DaemonConfig includes profiles section with definitions array and defaultProfileId
- Zod schemas validate profile data correctly
- Default profile definition included in DEFAULT_CONFIG in config.ts
- All existing tests still pass","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ben@thepuddle.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:14:47Z","created_by":"Benjamin Kitt","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:14:47Z"}

{"id":"mission-control-vug.1","title":"Define Profile types and schema","description":"Define the TypeScript types for Claude Code Profiles and add Zod validation schemas.

## What to do
1. Add `Profile` interface to `mission-control/scripts/daemon/types.ts`:
   ```ts
   export interface Profile {
     id: string;           // unique identifier (e.g., 'default', 'openai-compat', 'local-llm')
     name: string;         // display name
     description?: string; // optional description
     env: Record<string, string>; // environment variables to set when spawning Claude Code
   }
   ```

2. Add `ProfilesConfig` to `DaemonConfig` type:
   ```ts
   profiles: {
     definitions: Profile[];
     defaultProfileId: string; // defaults to 'default'
   };
   ```

3. Add `Profile` type to `mission-control/src/lib/types.ts` (mirrors daemon types for the frontend)

4. Add Zod schemas to `mission-control/src/lib/validations.ts`:
   - profileCreateSchema
   - profileUpdateSchema
   - Update daemonConfigUpdateSchema to include profiles section

5. The 'default' profile should have an empty env object (plain Claude Code behavior)

## Files to modify
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/types.ts
- mission-control/src/lib/types.ts
- mission-control/src/lib/validations.ts

## Acceptance criteria
- Profile interface defined with id, name, description, env fields
- DaemonConfig includes profiles section with definitions array and defaultProfileId
- Zod schemas validate profile data correctly
- Default profile definition included in DEFAULT_CONFIG in config.ts
- All existing tests still pass","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Benjamin Kitt","owner":"ben@thepuddle.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:14:47Z","created_by":"Benjamin Kitt","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:17:01Z"}

{"id":"mission-control-vug.2","title":"Add profiles section to daemon config with defaults","description":"Update the daemon config system to include a profiles section with a built-in default profile.

## What to do
1. Update `DEFAULT_CONFIG` in `mission-control/scripts/daemon/config.ts` to include:
   ```ts
   profiles: {
     definitions: [
       {
         id: 'default',
         name: 'Claude Code (Default)',
         description: 'Standard Claude Code with no additional configuration',
         env: {},
       }
     ],
     defaultProfileId: 'default',
   }
   ```

2. Update `validateConfig()` in config.ts to merge/validate the profiles section:
   - Validate each profile has a valid id (alphanumeric + hyphens)
   - Validate env keys are valid environment variable names
   - Validate defaultProfileId references an existing profile
   - Preserve the default profile if not overridden

3. Add a helper function `getProfile(profileId: string): Profile | null` to config.ts

4. Ensure backward compatibility: if daemon-config.json has no profiles section, the default is injected automatically

## Files to modify
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/config.ts

## Acceptance criteria
- Default config includes a 'default' profile with empty env
- validateConfig correctly merges and validates profiles
- getProfile helper resolves profiles by ID
- Existing daemon-config.json without profiles section still loads correctly
- Config validation rejects invalid profile IDs and env var names","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ben@thepuddle.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:14:56Z","created_by":"Benjamin Kitt","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:14:56Z"}

{"id":"mission-control-vug.3","title":"Add profileId to AgentDefinition","description":"Add an optional profileId field to the agent definition so agents can be assigned to specific profiles.

## What to do
1. Add `profileId?: string | null` to `AgentDefinition` in `mission-control/src/lib/types.ts`

2. Add `profileId` to the daemon's `AgentDef` interface in `mission-control/scripts/daemon/prompt-builder.ts`

3. Update `agentCreateSchema` in validations.ts:
   - Add `profileId: z.string().max(50).nullable().optional().default(null)`

4. Update `agentUpdateSchema` in validations.ts:
   - Add `profileId: z.string().max(50).nullable().optional()`

5. Update the agents API route (`mission-control/src/app/api/agents/route.ts`):
   - POST: include profileId in new agent creation
   - PUT: allow profileId updates

6. Existing agents without profileId should default to null (meaning use the system default profile)

## Files to modify
- mission-control/src/lib/types.ts (AgentDefinition interface)
- mission-control/src/lib/validations.ts (agent schemas)
- mission-control/src/app/api/agents/route.ts (POST/PUT handlers)
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/prompt-builder.ts (AgentDef interface)

## Acceptance criteria
- AgentDefinition has optional profileId field
- API accepts profileId on agent create/update
- Null/undefined profileId means 'use system default'
- Existing agents.json without profileId field loads without error
- Agent validation schemas accept the new field","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ben@thepuddle.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:05Z","created_by":"Benjamin Kitt","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:05Z"}

{"id":"mission-control-vug.3","title":"Add profileId to AgentDefinition","description":"Add an optional profileId field to the agent definition so agents can be assigned to specific profiles.

## What to do
1. Add `profileId?: string | null` to `AgentDefinition` in `mission-control/src/lib/types.ts`

2. Add `profileId` to the daemon's `AgentDef` interface in `mission-control/scripts/daemon/prompt-builder.ts`

3. Update `agentCreateSchema` in validations.ts:
   - Add `profileId: z.string().max(50).nullable().optional().default(null)`

4. Update `agentUpdateSchema` in validations.ts:
   - Add `profileId: z.string().max(50).nullable().optional()`

5. Update the agents API route (`mission-control/src/app/api/agents/route.ts`):
   - POST: include profileId in new agent creation
   - PUT: allow profileId updates

6. Existing agents without profileId should default to null (meaning use the system default profile)

## Files to modify
- mission-control/src/lib/types.ts (AgentDefinition interface)
- mission-control/src/lib/validations.ts (agent schemas)
- mission-control/src/app/api/agents/route.ts (POST/PUT handlers)
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/prompt-builder.ts (AgentDef interface)

## Acceptance criteria
- AgentDefinition has optional profileId field
- API accepts profileId on agent create/update
- Null/undefined profileId means 'use system default'
- Existing agents.json without profileId field loads without error
- Agent validation schemas accept the new field","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Benjamin Kitt","owner":"ben@thepuddle.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:05Z","created_by":"Benjamin Kitt","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:55:25Z"}

{"id":"mission-control-vug.4","title":"Wire profile env vars into runner's buildSafeEnv","description":"Modify the agent runner to resolve the profile for an agent and inject profile environment variables into the spawned Claude Code process.

## What to do
1. Update `buildSafeEnv()` in `mission-control/scripts/daemon/security.ts` to accept a `profileEnv` parameter:
   ```ts
   export function buildSafeEnv(opts?: { 
     agentTeams?: boolean; 
     profileEnv?: Record<string, string>;
   }): Record<string, string>
   ```
   After building the base safe env, merge `profileEnv` entries on top.

2. Update `SpawnOptions` in types.ts to include an optional `profileEnv?: Record<string, string>`

3. Update `AgentRunner.spawnAgent()` in runner.ts:
   - Pass `opts.profileEnv` to `buildSafeEnv()`

4. Update `Dispatcher.dispatchTask()` in dispatcher.ts:
   - Before spawning, resolve the agent's profileId
   - Look up the profile from config (`getProfile()`)
   - Fall back to the defaultProfileId if agent has no profileId
   - Pass profile.env to spawnAgent via SpawnOptions

5. Update `run-task.ts` main() function:
   - Same profile resolution logic as dispatcher
   - Pass profile.env when calling runner.spawnAgent()

6. Security consideration: Log which profile is being used (name/id only, NOT env var values) for auditability

## Files to modify
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/security.ts (buildSafeEnv)
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/types.ts (SpawnOptions)
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/runner.ts (spawnAgent)
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/dispatcher.ts (dispatchTask, runScheduledCommand)
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/run-task.ts (main)

## Acceptance criteria
- Profile env vars are injected into spawned Claude Code processes
- Default profile (empty env) produces identical behavior to current code
- Profile env vars override system env vars when there's a conflict
- Profile name/id is logged when spawning (but NOT env values — security)
- Existing functionality without profiles works identically","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ben@thepuddle.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:18Z","created_by":"Benjamin Kitt","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:18Z"}

{"id":"mission-control-vug.5","title":"Support mission-level profile overrides","description":"Allow missions to override the profile assigned to agents when running within a mission context.

## What to do
1. Add optional `profileOverrides` to `MissionRun` type in `mission-control/scripts/daemon/types.ts`:
   ```ts
   profileOverrides?: Record<string, string>; // agentId → profileId
   ```
   And mirror in `mission-control/src/lib/types.ts`.

2. Update the missions API route (`mission-control/src/app/api/missions/route.ts`) to accept profileOverrides when starting a mission.

3. Update the project run API (`mission-control/src/app/api/projects/[id]/run/route.ts`) to pass profileOverrides when starting a mission.

4. Update profile resolution in `run-task.ts` and `dispatcher.ts`:
   - When a mission is active, check `mission.profileOverrides[agentId]` first
   - Fall back to agent's own profileId
   - Fall back to system defaultProfileId

5. Profile resolution priority (highest to lowest):
   1. Mission-level override for this agent
   2. Agent's own profileId
   3. System defaultProfileId

## Files to modify
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/types.ts (MissionRun)
- mission-control/src/lib/types.ts (MissionRun)
- mission-control/src/app/api/missions/route.ts
- mission-control/src/app/api/projects/[id]/run/route.ts
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/run-task.ts
- mission-control/scripts/daemon/dispatcher.ts

## Acceptance criteria
- Missions can specify profile overrides per agent
- Override takes precedence over agent's own profile
- Resolution cascade: mission override → agent profile → system default
- Existing missions without profileOverrides work identically
- Profile override is logged when used","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ben@thepuddle.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:29Z","created_by":"Benjamin Kitt","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:29Z"}

{"id":"mission-control-vug.7","title":"Add unit tests for profile system","description":"Write comprehensive unit tests for the profile system covering config loading, validation, env var injection, and profile resolution.

## What to do
1. Add tests to `mission-control/__tests__/` (new file: `profiles.test.ts`):

2. Test config loading:
   - Default config has 'default' profile with empty env
   - Config without profiles section loads with defaults
   - Custom profiles are preserved through validation
   - Invalid profile IDs are rejected
   - Invalid env var names are rejected
   - defaultProfileId must reference an existing profile

3. Test buildSafeEnv with profile env:
   - Empty profileEnv produces standard safe env (no change)
   - Profile env vars are merged into safe env
   - Profile env vars override safe env entries when conflicting
   - Agent teams + profile env work together

4. Test profile resolution:
   - Agent with profileId → that profile
   - Agent without profileId → system default
   - Mission override → overrides agent profile
   - Missing profile → falls back to default
   - getProfile helper returns correct profile or null

5. Test validation schemas:
   - profileCreateSchema accepts valid profiles
   - profileCreateSchema rejects invalid data
   - agentCreateSchema/agentUpdateSchema accept profileId
   - daemonConfigUpdateSchema accepts profiles section

## Files to create/modify
- mission-control/__tests__/profiles.test.ts (new)
- mission-control/__tests__/security.test.ts (extend buildSafeEnv tests)

## Acceptance criteria
- All new tests pass
- Tests cover happy path and edge cases
- Tests verify backward compatibility (no profiles = default behavior)
- No regression in existing tests","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ben@thepuddle.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:47Z","created_by":"Benjamin Kitt","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:47Z"}

{"id":"mission-control-vug.8","title":"Add Profiles API endpoint","description":"Create a REST API endpoint for managing profiles (CRUD operations on daemon-config.json profiles section).

## What to do
1. Create new API route: `mission-control/src/app/api/profiles/route.ts`

2. Implement handlers:
   - GET: List all profiles (from daemon config)
   - POST: Create a new profile (add to daemon-config.json profiles.definitions)
   - PUT: Update an existing profile
   - DELETE: Delete a profile (cannot delete 'default' profile; reassign agents first)

3. Profile operations should read/write to daemon-config.json's profiles section using the existing config load/save pattern.

4. Validation:
   - Profile ID must be unique
   - Cannot delete a profile that is assigned to agents (return 409 with list of affected agents)
   - Cannot delete the defaultProfileId profile
   - Env var keys must be valid (alphanumeric + underscores, no empty strings)

5. Use existing data helpers pattern from `mission-control/src/lib/data.ts` for thread-safe file access.

## Files to create/modify
- mission-control/src/app/api/profiles/route.ts (new)
- mission-control/src/lib/data.ts (if needed for daemon config mutation helper)

## Acceptance criteria
- CRUD operations work for profiles
- Cannot delete default profile or profiles in use
- Validation rejects invalid profile data
- API follows same patterns as existing endpoints (agents, tasks, etc.)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ben@thepuddle.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:57Z","created_by":"Benjamin Kitt","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:57Z"}

- Validation rejects invalid profile data
- API follows same patterns as existing endpoints (agents, tasks, etc.)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Benjamin Kitt","owner":"ben@thepuddle.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:15:57Z","created_by":"Benjamin Kitt","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:28:12Z"}
